title: Stone Cold
editorialSummary: Gemstones that can plausibly be sorted by color, mineral family, OR birthstone month — all three sorting principles are simultaneously available, making every group feel unstable until you fix the correct axis (mineral family). False trail 1: Ruby, Garnet, and Red Spinel are all red — players will try to group them together, but Ruby is corundum, Garnet is a separate mineral group, and Red Spinel is magnesium aluminium oxide. False trail 2: Sapphire and Ruby are both corundum (aluminum oxide), so they must share the corundum group even though they are different colors and different months. False trail 3: Aquamarine and Emerald are both beryl — players who don't know mineralogy will separate them by color (blue vs. green). False trail 4: Topaz is often confused with quartz (citrine looks similar in color), but Topaz is aluminum silicate fluoride while Citrine is quartz — placed in separate mineral groups. Note: all mineral family assignments are verifiable in the Gemological Institute of America (GIA) standard references.
